---
title: "Projects and Funding Context"
page-layout: full
---

My research has developed through two connected scientific environments: the Topological Field Theory of Data programme at the University of Camerino and the Mathematical, Computational and Experimental Neuroscience environment at the Basque Center for Applied Mathematics.

---

## BioShape & Data Science Lab and Topological Field Theory of Data

::: {.card}
::: {.card-body}

My doctoral research was developed in the [BioShape & Data Science Lab](https://www.emanuelamerelli.eu/bigdata/) at the University of Camerino, in the scientific environment shaped by [Emanuela Merelli](https://www.emanuelamerelli.eu/), [Mario Rasetti](https://www.polito.it/en/staff?p=mario.rasetti), and the Topological Field Theory of Data programme.

This environment shaped my interest in mathematical and computational modelling of complex systems, quantum foundations, quantum information and computation, topology-driven methods, categorical and geometric structures, and their applications to data, computation, and biological systems.

A central reference for this scientific line is [*Topological Field Theory of Data: Mining Data Beyond Complex Networks*](https://doi.org/10.1017/9781316403877.002), by Mario Rasetti and Emanuela Merelli.

### Related work

- *Contextual Semantics Machinery* — PhD thesis.
- *Contextual Semantics Machinery* — public repository manuscript with Emanuela Merelli.
- *The Bundle-Theoretic Structure and Computational Modeling of Quantum Contextuality*.
- *The Gauge-Theoretic Structure of Non-Locality and Contextuality*.

:::
:::

---

## TOPDRIM — Topology Driven Methods for Complex Systems

::: {.card}
::: {.card-body}

[TOPDRIM](https://www.topdrim.eu/), *Topology Driven Methods for Complex Systems*, was an EU FP7/FET-Proactive project coordinated by the University of Camerino under grant agreement `318121`. The project developed topology-driven mathematical and computational methods for multi-level complex systems, connecting topology, geometry, statistical physics, information theory, computer science, and biology.

My doctoral work and later contextual-semantics research are connected to this scientific lineage through the Topological Field Theory of Data line developed at Camerino.

### Useful links

- [TOPDRIM project website](https://www.topdrim.eu/)
- [TOPDRIM publications](https://www.topdrim.eu/publications/)
- [CORDIS TOPDRIM record](https://cordis.europa.eu/project/id/318121)

### Related work

- *Contextual Semantics Machinery* — PhD thesis.
- *Contextual Semantics Machinery* — public repository manuscript.
- *The Bundle-Theoretic Structure and Computational Modeling of Quantum Contextuality*.

:::
:::

---

## BCAM Mathematical, Computational and Experimental Neuroscience Environment

::: {.card}
::: {.card-body}

My current postdoctoral work is based at the [Basque Center for Applied Mathematics](https://www.bcamath.org/en), within the Mathematical, Computational and Experimental Neuroscience research environment led by [Serafim Rodrigues](https://www.bcamath.org/en/people/bcam-members/srodrigues). This environment connects mathematical modelling, computational neuroscience, complex systems, topology-driven methods, and applications to biological and networked systems.

### Funding context

Across the papers and manuscripts where explicitly acknowledged, my BCAM-based work has received support from:

- grant `PID2023-146683OB-I00`, funded by MICIU/AEI and ERDF, EU;
- Ikerbasque Foundation;
- Basque Government BERC 2022–2025 programme;
- BCAM Severo Ochoa accreditation `CEX2021-001142-S`;
- BCAM-IKUR, funded by the Basque Government, IKUR Strategy, and the European Union NextGenerationEU/PRTR;
- [ONBODY `KK-2023/00070`](https://www.bcamath.org/en/research/projects/onbody), funded by the Basque Government through the ELKARTEK programme;
- [MATHEMATICIANS `PID2023-146683OB-I00`](https://www.bcamath.org/en/research/projects/mathematicians), an AEI-funded BCAM project led by Serafim Rodrigues, where relevant.

### Related work

- *Understanding Entanglement Through the Lens of Quantifiable Algebraic Structures: Application to Bird Navigation*.
- *Constraint Semantics for Multi-Level Organisation*.
- *The Gauge-Theoretic Structure of Non-Locality and Contextuality*.

:::
:::

---

## Selected Ongoing Projects

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### The Evolving Reconfigurable Network Bundle: A Geometric Framework for Evolving Communication Systems

Public manuscript currently not available.

This project develops a geometric framework for evolving communication systems in which network topology, configuration spaces, partial information, and admissible transformations evolve together. The central problem is dynamic feasibility: when can local configurations, paths, policies, or updates be assembled into globally coherent behaviour under changing network constraints?

**Research areas:** evolving networks; communication systems; reconfigurable and heterogeneous infrastructures; partial information; dynamic feasibility; structural diagnostics.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Optical Network Feasibility, Partial Information, and Structural Diagnostics

Ongoing collaborative project with Serafim Rodrigues and Mathieu Desroches.

This project studies optical-network feasibility under partial information and changing constraints. The aim is to connect local optical feasibility, network-level routing, incomplete access to system state, and structural diagnostics within a coherent mathematical framework for communication networks.

**Research areas:** optical networks; network feasibility; partial observability; structural diagnostics; communication-system modelling; evolving network theory.

:::
:::
:::

:::

---

## Related Pages

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [Research](research.qmd)

Research areas, methods, and current directions.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [Publications](publications.qmd)

Peer-reviewed publications, theses, preprints, and public manuscripts.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [Contact](contact.qmd)

Affiliation, email, and academic links.

:::
:::
:::

:::
